channel.title: AP Physics C Knowledge Graph
channel.description: Last 10 notes on AP Physics C Knowledge Graph

item.title: Index
item.description: Energy This section covers energy concepts in mechanics, including conservation of energy and its applications.

item.title: Example 6 - Sliding Rope
item.description: Example 6: Sliding Rope on Frictionless Table Problem Statement A uniform rope of length L and mass M is placed on a frictionless table, with one end of the rope hanging slightly over the edge.

item.title: Example 7 - Ball in Tunnel Through Planet
item.description: Example 7: Ball in Tunnel Through Planet Problem Statement A spherical, nonrotating planet has a radius R and a uniform density \rho throughout its volume.

item.title: Example 8 - Power from Position Function
item.description: Example 8: Power and Work from Position Function Problem Statement The position of a 280-g object is given (in meters) by: x = 4.0t^3 - 8.0t^2 - 44t where t is in seconds.

item.title: Example 9 - Potential Energy and Turning Points
item.description: Example 9: Potential Energy and Turning Points Problem Statement A particle moves where its potential energy is given by: U(r) = U_0\left[\left(\frac{2}{r^2}\right) - \left(\frac{1}{r}\right)\right] where r is the radial distance and U_0 is a positive constant with units of energy.

item.title: Index
item.description: Examples - AP Physics C Mechanics This folder contains worked examples from AP Physics C Mechanics free response questions.

item.title: Example 1 - Sphere with Air Resistance
item.description: Example 1: Sphere with Velocity-Dependent Air Resistance Problem Statement A small sphere of mass m is launched straight upward from the top of a high cliff, as shown in Figure 1, with an initial speed v_0 at time t = 0.

item.title: Example 17 - Springs in Series and Parallel
item.description: Example 17: Springs in Series and Parallel Problem Statement A mass m is connected to two springs, with spring constants k_1 and k_2, in two different ways as shown in Figure 17.

item.title: Example 18 - Torsional Oscillation of Meter Stick
item.description: Example 18: Torsional Oscillation of a Meter Stick Problem Statement A meter stick is hung at its center from a thin wire.

item.title: Example 19 - Ballistic Measurement with Spring
item.description: Example 19: Ballistic Measurement with Spring Problem Statement Agent Arlene devised the following method of measuring the muzzle velocity of a rifle.
